name: Amoreira Trail
name_original: Trilho dos Pescadores — percurso circular na Costa Vicentina
description: Jog the Amoreira Trail, a 4.5-mile moderate loop near Aljezur, offering diverse coastal and inland scenery with ocean views.
region_intro: Jogging around Aljezur offers varied terrain within the Southwest Alentejo and Vicentine Coast Natural Park. The region features a dramatic coastline with high cliffs, rugged shores, and distinctive black rocks. Inland, rolling hills provide diverse landscapes for running, complemented by the Ribeira de Aljezur river. The extensive Rota Vicentina network, including the coastal Fishermen's Trail and the inland Historical Way, provides hundreds of kilometers of paths suitable for jogging. Best jogging routes around Aljezur The most popular jogging route is Amoreira Trail, a 4.5 miles (7.3 km) trail that takes about 51 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers diverse coastal and inland scenery with ocean views. Another top favourite among local runners is Monte Clérigo Beach – Abandoned Dinosaur Mini-Golf loop from Praia da Arrifana, a moderate 7.3 miles (11.7 km) path. This route caters to those seeking a longer run with varied coastal landscapes. Local runners also love the Arrifana Obelisk loop from Praia da Arrifana, a 4.5 miles (7.3 km) trail leading through coastal areas, often completed in about 49 minutes. Jogging around Aljezur is defined by coastal paths, rolling hills, and river valleys. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy loops to more challenging moderate routes. The routes in Aljezur are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 60 reviews. More than 1500 runners have used komoot to explore Aljezur's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Aljezur?
A: Aljezur offers a wide variety of running experiences, with over 100 routes available. These range from easy loops to more challenging moderate trails, ensuring options for different ability levels.

Q: What types of terrain can I expect on jogging routes around Aljezur?
A: Jogging routes in Aljezur feature diverse terrain. You'll find coastal paths with dramatic cliffs and rugged shores, rolling hills inland, and paths along river valleys like the Ribeira de Aljezur. Many routes are part of the extensive Rota Vicentina network, offering both coastal and inland experiences.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly running routes in Aljezur?
A: Yes, Aljezur has several easy running routes suitable for beginners. While specific easy routes aren't detailed here, the region offers 12 routes classified as 'easy' within its network of over 100 trails. These typically feature less elevation gain and shorter distances.

Q: Can I find routes with scenic viewpoints or natural attractions?
A: Absolutely. Many running routes in Aljezur offer stunning views and pass by natural attractions. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1420629880/amoreira-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Amoreira Trail&lt;/a&gt; provides diverse coastal and inland scenery with ocean views. You can also find highlights like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/113067" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Praia de Odeceixe and Seixe River Mouth&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/212479" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Praia da Bordeira&lt;/a&gt; along various paths.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Aljezur?
A: Yes, many of the running routes in Aljezur are circular, providing convenient starting and ending points. Popular examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1420629880/amoreira-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Amoreira Trail&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19317920" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte Clérigo Beach – Abandoned Dinosaur Mini-Golf loop&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21821710" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arrifana Obelisk loop&lt;/a&gt;, all designed as loops.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Aljezur?
A: The running trails in Aljezur are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.4 stars from over 60 reviews. Runners often praise the varied terrain, the stunning coastal views, and the well-maintained paths that make for an enjoyable experience.

Q: Are the running routes in Aljezur suitable for families?
A: While many routes are moderate, there are options suitable for families, especially those looking for shorter, less strenuous runs. The region's diverse landscape means you can find paths that are relatively flat and safe, particularly along some of the inland sections or wider coastal trails. Always check the route details for distance and elevation to ensure it matches your family's fitness level.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the jogging trails in Aljezur?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on many of the trails in Aljezur, especially those within the Southwest Alentejo and Vicentine Coast Natural Park. However, it's always advisable to keep your dog on a leash, particularly in protected areas, near wildlife, or on popular routes. Be mindful of local regulations, especially concerning beaches during peak season.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Aljezur?
A: The best time to go running in Aljezur is typically during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, the weather is milder and more pleasant, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the heavier rains of winter. The natural landscape is also particularly vibrant during spring.

Q: Are there any longer or more challenging running routes for experienced runners?
A: Yes, Aljezur offers several longer and more challenging routes. The region has 14 routes classified as 'difficult' and 77 as 'moderate'. The extensive Rota Vicentina, including the Fishermen's Trail and Historical Way, provides hundreds of kilometers of paths, with options like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19317920" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte Clérigo Beach – Abandoned Dinosaur Mini-Golf loop&lt;/a&gt; (11.7 km) or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21822402" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruins of Ribat da Arrifana – Monte Clérigo Beach loop&lt;/a&gt; (11.1 km) offering a good workout with varied coastal landscapes.

Q: Where can I find parking near the running trails?
A: Parking is generally available near popular trailheads and beaches in Aljezur. For routes starting near coastal areas like Praia da Arrifana or Praia de Monte Clérigo, you'll often find designated parking lots. In Aljezur town, there are also parking options that provide access to inland trails and the Rota Vicentina sections.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or cultural sites along the running paths?
A: Yes, the region blends natural beauty with historical elements. The historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5590420" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aljezur Castle&lt;/a&gt;, perched above the town, is accessible via a winding road that is part of the Rota Vicentina and offers spectacular views. Some routes may also pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21822402" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruins of Ribat da Arrifana&lt;/a&gt;, adding a historical dimension to your run.
tips: One of the most beautiful beaches in the southern part of the Western Algarve. Easy to reach, flat beach, you can swim well even when the waves are high. And if you're afraid of the waves in the Atlantic, just go swimming on the banks of the Ribera de Aljezur - absolutely quiet and fine sand. There is a nice beach restaurant (Paraiso Do Mar). We were there at the end of September and the end of May - absolutely quiet and only a few visitors. You can go hiking in the dunes and on the river banks and there is also a small nature trail.

The spectacular Praia da Carreagem is one of the most beautiful on the Costa Vicentina. From this viewpoint it is possible to admire the beach with a unique view. You can access the beach via a wooden staircase just ahead, near the car park.

At this point on the fishermen's trail, the route passes through the dunes and sand road. In addition to being very beautiful, it is a challenge for the imagination to remember that it is just a few kilometers from the coast.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to jog the Amoreira Trail?
faqs.answer: The Amoreira Trail is approximately 7.25 kilometers (4.5 miles) long. While hikers might take up to 2.5 hours, joggers can expect to complete it in about 50 minutes to 1.5 hours, depending on their pace and how often they stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: Is the Amoreira Trail suitable for beginner joggers?
faqs.answer: The trail is rated as moderate in difficulty. While some sections feature soft sand and can be steep or rough, other parts offer firmer ground. Beginners with a reasonable level of fitness should find it manageable, but sturdy trail running shoes are recommended due to the varied terrain.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Amoreira Trail?
faqs.answer: Parking is generally available near the starting point of the trail, particularly close to Amoreira Beach. It's advisable to arrive earlier in the day, especially during peak seasons, to secure a spot.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain will I encounter on this jogging route?
faqs.answer: The route offers a diverse mix of surfaces. You'll jog on soft sand paths, especially near Amoreira Beach and leading up to the cliffs. There are also sections with harder ground, loamy soil in the inland areas, and some steep, rough paths. Be prepared for varied conditions underfoot.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog the Amoreira Trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is enjoyable year-round, but spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) offer the most pleasant temperatures for jogging. During these seasons, the weather is milder, and the natural landscape is particularly vibrant. Summer can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon jogs are best.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the Amoreira Trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, dogs are generally permitted on the Amoreira Trail. However, as it's part of the Parque Natural do Sudoeste Alentejano e Costa Vicentina and intersects with the Fishermen's Trail (Rota Vicentina), it's important to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or near cliffs, and to clean up after them.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to access the trail or the Natural Park?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to jog the Amoreira Trail or to enter the Parque Natural do Sudoeste Alentejano e Costa Vicentina. However, visitors are expected to respect the natural environment and follow park regulations.

faqs.question: What scenic highlights can I expect along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers stunning and varied scenery. You'll experience the expansive sands of Amoreira Beach, breathtaking ocean views from atop the cliffs, and a beautiful View of Carreagem Beach. Inland, the route winds through hills with Mediterranean shrubs, pine forests, and marshland rich in biodiversity.

faqs.question: Does the Amoreira Trail connect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Amoreira Trail is part of the larger Rota Vicentina network, specifically designated as Route 16, the Praia da Amoreira beach circuit. The route also passes through sections of the Estação da Biodiversidade da Praia da Amoreira, the Historical Way, and the Rota Vicentina - Fishermen's Trail.

faqs.question: What's an insider tip for jogging the Amoreira Trail?
faqs.answer: To make the most of your jog, consider starting early in the morning. This allows you to experience the tranquil beauty of Amoreira Beach and the coastal cliffs before it gets busy, and you'll enjoy cooler temperatures, especially during warmer months. Also, be mindful of the soft sand sections near the beach, which can be more challenging for continuous jogging.
